description: Industry benchmark DPO (Days Payable Outstanding) by spend category and industry tier.

IT.notes: Software/SaaS often net-30 by contract; hardware net-45 to net-60 achievable

PROFESSIONAL_SERVICES.notes: Consulting firms typically net-30; pushing to net-45 feasible for large engagements

FACILITIES.notes: Utilities often fixed; maintenance and FM services net-45 achievable

MARKETING.notes: Agency retainers often net-30; project work can push to net-45

LOGISTICS.notes: Freight and 3PL benchmark around net-30 to net-45

TRAVEL.notes: Corporate card programs settle quickly; focus on corporate card DPO vs direct billing

HR.notes: RPO and benefits admin net-30 standard; benchmarks achievable at net-45

CONTINGENT_WORKFORCE.notes: Staffing agencies often require weekly/bi-weekly pay; net-30 is the stretch target

TELECOMMUNICATIONS.notes: Telco contracts often fixed; consolidation to one provider enables better terms

OFFICE_SUPPLIES.notes: Office supply distributors routinely offer net-45 to net-60 for volume accounts

INSURANCE.notes: Insurance premiums are typically fixed-schedule; limited DPO leverage

OTHER.notes: Default target; apply category-specific benchmarks where possible

working_capital_notes: DPO extension benefit = (target_dpo - current_dpo) / 365 * annual_category_spend. This is a one-time working capital release, not an ongoing P&L saving. At a 10% WACC, the annual cash flow value = working_capital_released * wacc.
